web.markdown:
# News [RSS](https://maplibre.org/news/index.xml)

[**MapLibre Newsletter February 2026** \\
\\
Welcome to the February 2026 edition of MapLibre Newsletter! It's been an active month with exciting updates, from shifting rendering backends to launching a collection of to perform MapLibre related tasks. A special thanks to MapTiler for their continued support. We updated our page this month, to make…\\
\\
Mar 3, 2026](https://maplibre.org/news/2026-03-03-maplibre-newsletter-february-2026/) [**Thank You for Powering MapLibre** \\
\\
MapLibre exists because organizations and individuals believe in open-source mapping infrastructure. Today we want to thank the sponsors who make that possible, share what we accomplished together in 2025, and explain what we need now. TL;DR 🫵 we need YOU. So first... MapLibre's Sponsorship Program is the backbone of our…\\
\\
Feb 18, 2026](https://maplibre.org/news/2026-02-18-supporting-maplibre/) [**CalTopo joins the MapLibre Sponsorship program** \\
\\
We’re pleased to announce that is now a of the MapLibre project. CalTopo has built one of the most trusted field mapping platforms in the US, relied on by search and rescue teams, wildland firefighters, and backcountry adventurers who need maps to work when it counts. We're proud…\\
\\
Feb 16, 2026](https://maplibre.org/news/2026-02-16-caltopo-announcement/) [**MapLibre Newsletter January 2026** \\
\\
Welcome to the January 2026 edition of MapLibre Newsletter! This edition covers project updates across the MapLibre ecosystem, a round-up of events from the past month, and highlights from our growing global community. MapLibre continues to move forward thanks to the support of its community. Since launching , 87 sponsors…\\
\\
Feb 3, 2026](https://maplibre.org/news/2026-02-03-maplibre-newsletter-january-2026/) [**Announcing MapLibre Tile: a modern and efficient vector tile format** \\
\\
Today we are happy to announce MapLibre Tile (MLT), a new modern and efficient vector tile format. MapLibre Tile (MLT) is a successor to .\\
It has been redesigned from the ground up to address the challenges of rapidly growing geospatial data volumes\\
and complex next-generation geospatial source formats, as well as…\\
\\
Jan 23, 2026](https://maplibre.org/news/2026-01-23-mlt-release/) [**MapLibre Newsletter December 2025** \\
\\
As we close out 2025, the MapLibre ecosystem continues to move forward across platforms, tooling, and the broader open-source mapping community. While December is traditionally a quieter month, there’s still plenty to share about performance improvements and new capabilities in MapLibre Native, GL JS, and other community-led initiatives. None of…\\
\\
Jan 3, 2026](https://maplibre.org/news/2026-01-03-maplibre-newsletter-december-2025/) [**MapLibre Newsletter November 2025** \\
\\
We open this edition by acknowledging AWS for their continued support of MapLibre during 2023–2025. Their contribution plays an important role in sustaining the growth of our open-source ecosystem. The highlight of this month is the landmark release of Martin v1.0, a major milestone for the project. We also bring…\\
\\
Dec 2, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-12-02-maplibre-newsletter-november-2025/) [**MapLibre Newsletter October 2025** \\
\\
We begin this edition with sincere appreciation for , whose ongoing sponsorship helps sustain the MapLibre community.\\
Welcome to the MapLibre Newsletter, October 2025 edition! Before diving into the updates, we’ve added a new .github repository, giving the MapLibre GitHub organization a fresh, welcoming README! Check it out here: https://github.com/maplibre We…\\
\\
Nov 4, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-11-04-maplibre-newsletter-october-2025/) [**MapLibre Newsletter September 2025** \\
\\
Welcome to the MapLibre Newsletter, September 2025 edition! This edition features advances in MapLibre Native, GL JS, Flutter, Martin, and more. Plus, we’ve given a fresh redesign. Go check it out to see what’s in progress, what’s under consideration, and what’s been released.\\
A big thanks to for driving…\\
\\
Oct 4, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-10-04-maplibre-newsletter-september-2025/) [**Results of the 2025 MapLibre Governing Board Election** \\
\\
We are happy to announce the results of the 2025 MapLibre Governing Board election and also welcome our newest Voting Members. A big thanks to everyone who participated in this year's election process.\\
Your engagement is what keeps MapLibre a strong, community-driven project. The following members have been elected to serve…\\
\\
Sep 22, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-09-22-board-elections-2025/) [**MapLibre Newsletter August 2025** \\
\\
August was a busy month for the MapLibre community. From welcoming new Voting Members as part of the Governing Board election process to new releases across key projects, there is plenty of progress to celebrate. First and foremost, we extend our gratitude to for continuing, and increasing their sponsorship…\\
\\
Sep 4, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-09-04-maplibre-newsletter-august-2025/) [**MapLibre Newsletter July 2025** \\
\\
Welcome to the July edition of the MapLibre newsletter! We start with a note of gratitude to our sponsor . This month's newsletter brings exciting updates across the MapLibre ecosystem, from enhanced font support for complex scripts in Native, a new frontend for Martin, to our growing presence at global…\\
\\
Aug 4, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-08-04-maplibre-newsletter-july-2025/) [**MapLibre Newsletter June 2025** \\
\\
Welcome to the June edition of the MapLibre newsletter! This month's roundup includes new feature updates in MapLibre Native and Web, along with milestones in projects like the SwiftUI DSL and Compose Playground. We’re also pleased to share that the now supports GitHub Codespaces, making it easier to get…\\
\\
Jul 2, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-07-02-maplibre-newsletter-june-2025/) [**MapLibre Newsletter May 2025** \\
\\
Welcome to the May edition of the MapLibre newsletter! This month's updates range from seamless Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF support in GL JS to fresh releases for Martin Tile Server and native SDKs, and the addition of a new team member. A special thanks to for renewing their sponsorship for…\\
\\
Jun 2, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-06-02-maplibre-newsletter-may-2025/) [**MapLibre Newsletter April 2025** \\
\\
Welcome to the April edition of the MapLibre Monthly! We’re excited to share major updates across Native, GL JS, and Martin - ranging from performance optimizations to new extensibility features.\\
And before we dive in, a heartfelt thanks to for renewing their sponsorship! We released two versions this month: Looking…\\
\\
May 4, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-05-04-maplibre-newsletter-april-2025/) [**MapLibre Newsletter March 2025** \\
\\
March has been a busy month across the MapLibre ecosystem, with new releases, major community contributions, and powerful features in the works. We’re also incredibly grateful that . MapTiler has been a long-time supporter of open-source mapping, and their sponsorship helps ensure the sustainability and growth of our community. With…\\
\\
Apr 4, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-04-04-maplibre-newsletter-march-2025/) [**MapTiler becomes a MapLibre Silver Sponsor** \\
\\
We are excited to announce that MapTiler has joined the MapLibre project as a Silver Sponsor! is a global provider of mapping services and software tools, recognized for their strong commitment to the open-source mapping ecosystem. They are the original creators and primary maintainers of the OpenMapTiles project, and…\\
\\
Mar 31, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-03-31-maptiler-announcement/) [**MapLibre Newsletter February 2025** \\
\\
We’re excited to share key updates across MapLibre Native, GL JS, Flutter, and the MapLibre Tile Specification (MLT), along with details on upcoming community meetings. A huge thank you to everyone who supports MapLibre through and platforms 🎉. Through these channels, we received $4,644.23 in 2023, and in…\\
\\
Mar 3, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-03-03-maplibre-newsletter-february-2025/) [**MapLibre Newsletter January 2025** \\
\\
As we step into 2025, we want to start with a heartfelt thank you to AWS for renewing their sponsorship for MapLibre. Their ongoing commitment since 2022 has played a vital role in the growth, and strengthening of our global community. In addition to AWS, we want to extend our…\\
\\
Feb 3, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-02-03-maplibre-newsletter-january-2025/) [**MapLibre Newsletter December 2024** \\
\\
Happy New Year, MapLibre Community! We are thrilled to share big updates for MapLibre Native (including Vulkan support), the official launch of MapLibre GL JS Version 5.0, new features in Martin Tile Server, and more. In January we will hold a MapLibre Eastern Call again to better connect with contributors…\\
\\
Jan 5, 2025](https://maplibre.org/news/2025-01-05-maplibre-newsletter-december-2024/) [**MapLibre Android with Vulkan Support Released** \\
\\
We are excited to announce the official release of MapLibre Android with Vulkan support! Vulkan is a modern graphics API which brings advantages such as improved performance, improved observability and better stability. Vulkan is poised to replace OpenGL ES eventually on Android. Starting 2026, new devices will only support OpenGL…\\
\\
Dec 12, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-12-12-maplibre-android-vulkan/) [**MapLibre Newsletter November 2024** \\
\\
November month has seen a lot of updates from across different projects in Maplibre ecosystem. From multiple pre-releases for MapLibre GL JS and to groundbreaking updates in Maplibre Native, there's a lot to explore. In addition, a recent survey shed light on user feedback for flutter Maplibre GL. Don't forget…\\
\\
Dec 1, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-12-01-maplibre-newsletter-november-2024/) [**Team update and our ongoing commitment to the community** \\
\\
We’d like to share a recent update regarding our at MapLibre. One of our team members, who led our Marketing and Fundraising, has transitioned out of their role. We’re grateful for their efforts in fostering connections within our community and with our sponsors. We wish them well in their…\\
\\
Nov 12, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-11-12-team-updates/) [**MapLibre Newsletter October 2024** \\
\\
We’re delighted to begin the October newsletter with a gracious and hearty Thank You to Meta for their unwavering support as a sponsor of MapLibre in 2024! We look forward to what is to come. We have released versions 5.0.0-pre.2, 5.0.0-pre.3 and 5.0.0-pre.4 this month.\\
One interesting feature that was recently…\\
\\
Nov 5, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-10-30-maplibre-newsletter-october-2024/) [**Oliver Wipfli leaving the MapLibre Team**\\
\\
, MapLibre announced that Oliver would become the first person to work on the MapLibre team in the capacity of coordinator. Oliver has been involved with the project before that, being the reviewer of the now legendary for MapLibre GL JS, one of the first major milestones after the…\\
\\
Oct 11, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-10-11-farewell-oliver/) [**New People Joining the Maplibre Team** \\
\\
New people have joined the MapLibre Team. Please give them a warm welcome! Isaac Besora has joined MapLibre as Graphics Engineer. His initial focus will be improvements in MapLibre GL JS in particular in the areas of Terrain 3D and the new Globe feature. Isaac brings expertise in graphics programming…\\
\\
Oct 4, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-10-04-new-team-members/) [**MapLibre Newsletter September 2024** \\
\\
We’re delighted to begin the September newsletter with a heartfelt THANK YOU to Microsoft for their unwavering support as a sponsor of MapLibre in 2024! In this month three versions were released: 4.7.0, 4.7.1 and 5.0.0-pre.1. The pre-release of version 5 is meant to get some feedback on the newly…\\
\\
Sep 30, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-09-30-maplibre-newsletter-september-2024/) [**MapLibre Newsletter August 2024** \\
\\
We are glad to open the August newsletter with a cordial THANK YOU to for continuing to sponsor MapLibre in 2024! At the end of August, the MapLibre Governing Board election 2024 took place. The following people have been elected: Congratulations to the new and old Members of the…\\
\\
Sep 2, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-09-02-maplibre-newsletter-august-2024/) [**Governing Board Election 2024** \\
\\
The MapLibre Governing Board members serve for a one year term and at the end of August we hold the Board election with representatives of the community, the MapLibre Voting Members. Yesterday, on Thursday, Aug 29th, 2024, the election poll closed and the following people were elected to the Board:…\\
\\

\\
continues to support MapLibre in 2024 - Thank You!The election will take place on Thursday, August 29th, 2024. The Governing Board consists of 5 members which get elected by the MapLibre Voting Members. The primary role of the Board is to set the long-term strategy of MapLibre, overview MapLibre's…\\

Jul 31, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-07-31-maplibre-newsletter-july-2024/) [**MapLibre Newsletter June 2024** \\
\\
We would like to open the June newsletter by thanking for their continued support as a member of the MapLibre Sponsorship Program! In June 2024, we were also happy to welcome as a new Silver tier sponsor, see . Two minor versions were released: and . Some…\\
\\

\\
Mapme joins the MapLibre Sponsorship Program as a Silver Sponsor. The Silver tier of USD 10,000 will be used for general maintenance of the MapLibre codebases and project coordination. is the versatile tool for creating\\
interactive maps with no coding required. MapLibre is a core component of\\
Mapme's user-friendly platform,\\

Jun 28, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-06-28-mapme-announcement/) [**MapLibre Newsletter May 2024** \\
\\
Mierune, our first-ever sponsor, has made a donation again to support MapLibre in 2024 for the third year in a row. Thank you so much ! In a monthly MapLibre meeting several months ago, presented his research on a new vector tile format and how it might outperform conventional…\\
\\
May 31, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-05-31-maplibre-newsletter-may-2024/) [**MapLibre Newsletter April 2024** \\
\\
At the end of March, we were happy to announce that Mappedin, a global leader in indoor mapping and spatial data management, joined the MapLibre Sponsorship Program. Let us give a warm welcome to our latest sponsor Mappedin! In addition to the usual monthly meetings for MapLibre GL JS and…\\
\\
Apr 30, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-04-30-maplibre-newsletter-april-2024/) [**Mappedin joins the MapLibre Sponsorship Program** \\
\\
Mappedin joins the MapLibre Sponsorship Program at the Silver tier. With a yearly of USD $10,000, Mappedin supports the maintenance of MapLibre’s codebases and ensures a stable future for the project. By supporting MapLibre, Mappedin strengthens the open-source maps ecosystem and makes its map rendering technology available to everyone.\\
\\
Mar 26, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-03-26-mappedin-announcement/) [**MapLibre Newsletter March 2024** \\
\\
In March 2024, we onboarded into the MapLibre GitHub organization. MapLibre GL Inspect is a small plugin for MapLibre GL JS that allows you to inspect the contents of vector tiles including the attributes of vector tile features. Special thanks to who maintained this plugin over the last…\\
\\
Mar 25, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-03-25-maplibre-newsletter-march-2024/) [**MapLibre Newsletter February 2024** \\
\\
In February 2024, we were happy to that Microsoft has joined the MapLibre Sponsorship Program. With Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft we now have three companies that have a seat in the MapLibre Advisory Council which meets with members of the MapLibre Governing Board on a quarterly basis. MapLibre's fiscal…\\
\\
Feb 29, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-02-29-maplibre-newsletter-february-2024/) [**Microsoft joins the MapLibre Sponsorship Program** \\
\\
Microsoft joins the MapLibre Sponsorship Program as a Silver Sponsor. The Silver tier of USD 80,000 will be used to 20 percent for development of new features and to 80 percent for general maintenance of the MapLibre codebases and project coordination. From the very beginning Microsoft was instrumental in…\\
\\
Feb 20, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-02-20-msft-announcement/) [**MapLibre Newsletter January 2024** \\
\\
A lot of great things happened this January: Maputnik, Metal, new releases of MapLibre Native Node bindings and Martin, and hopefully our last prerelease of MapLibre GL JS v4 all appeared this month. We have published a prerelease for MapLibre GL JS v4, see . Unless there should be some…\\
\\
Jan 30, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-01-30-maplibre-newsletter-january-2024/) [**Metal Support for MapLibre Native iOS Is Here!** \\
\\
We are excited to announce the release of MapLibre Native for iOS v6.0.0 with Metal support! is a map rendering toolkit written in C++ that can be used on many different . For GPU accelerated real-time rendering, MapLibre Native traditionally relied only on the industry standard graphics API called…\\
\\
Jan 19, 2024](https://maplibre.org/news/2024-01-19-metal-support-for-maplibre-native-ios-is-here/) [**MapLibre Newsletter December 2023** \\
\\
MapLibre wishes you all the best for 2024! https://demotiles.maplibre.org 2023 was an interesting year for MapLibre. It was the first time that we had paid maintainers who took care of MapLibre GL JS and MapLibre Native. Harel Mazor and Bart Louwers are the main people who served in this role…\\
\\
Dec 30, 2023](https://maplibre.org/news/2023-12-30-maplibre-newsletter-december-2023/) [**MapLibre Newsletter November 2023** \\
\\
Versions 3.5.2, 3.6.0, 3.6.1 were released, and we started going into the pre-release mode for MapLibre GL JS v4. The most significant breaking change in version 4 will be the replacement of outdated JavaScript callbacks with Promises in several public APIs. You can now start testing the version 4 pre-releases…\\
\\
Nov 30, 2023](https://maplibre.org/news/2023-11-30-maplibre-newsletter-november-2023/) [**Meta continues to support MapLibre** \\
\\
Meta continues to support MapLibre and increases its donation from the Silver to the Gold tier in its second year of sponsorship. The Gold tier donation of USD 150,000 will be used to 25 percent for development of new features and to 75 percent for general maintenance of the MapLibre…\\
\\
Nov 8, 2023](https://maplibre.org/news/2023-11-08-meta-continues-to-support-maplibre/) [**MapLibre Newsletter October 2023** \\
\\
Just in time for this newsletter, MapLibre GL JS has gained over . Great that we got so far and thanks to everyone who contributed in the past and made this possible! In the last month, 5 releases of MapLibre GL JS were made. Below you find some highlights, for…\\
\\
Oct 30, 2023](https://maplibre.org/news/2023-10-30-maplibre-newsletter-october-2023/) [**MapLibre Newsletter September 2023** \\
\\
It is already one year now that MapLibre has an elected Governing Board, and as the Board members serve a one-year-term, it was time for new Governing Board Elections. We held the Elections and all existing Board members have been re-elected. Read more . MapLibre was represented at two events…\\
\\
Sep 18, 2023](https://maplibre.org/news/2023-09-18-maplibre-newsletter-september-2023/) [**Results of the 2023 MapLibre Governing Board Election** \\
\\
The MapLibre Governing Board Elections are finished - all existing Board members have been reelected. Congratulations to the elected Board members and a big thank you to Jonah Adkins for running for the Board as the only newcomer! Prior to the Governing Board Elections, 50 new Voting Members were nominated…\\
\\

\\
We want to thank each and every of our 92 (49 new!) for their vote and are looking forward to a next great year with our vibrant community. The result of the election will be published separately next week. The new modularized renderer . This happened in three stages:…\\

\\
Radar is joining the MapLibre Sponsorship Program as a Silver Sponsor. , CTO at Radar, was so kind to share a few insights into Radar and its relationship to MapLibre below: MapLibre: Radar is Location Infrastructure for mobile and web apps. What role does MapLibre play in your products? Tim:…\\

We want to emphasize the process of nominating Candidates for the Governing Board as well as new Voting Members and are looking forward to your suggestions. Please note the following timeline: Registrations for Candidates for the Governing Board close…\\

\\
We want to emphasize input of prototyping a new vector tile format called . has shared a demo using Harfbuzz to render complex text. Check out . The new Modularized renderer architecture based on Drawables is being merged. The will be merged into . The last improvement…\\

\\
We would like to thank JawgMaps for their trust. As you might have read in our , JawgMaps has joined the MapLibre Sponsorship Program and supports us as a Silver Sponsor! As part of the effort to implement a Metal backend for iOS, first the renderer needed to be made…\\

Jun 23, 2023](https://maplibre.org/news/2023-06-23-maplibre-newsletter-june-2023/) [**JawgMaps becomes a MapLibre Silver Sponsor** \\

We are happy to announce that JawgMaps has joined the MapLibre Sponsorship Program and supports us as a Silver Sponsor! The JawgMaps platform provides enterprise-grade mapping services built from both OpenSource and proprietary tools, as well as OpenStreetMap data. Far from simply being a map customizing tool, Jawg provides you…\\

\\
It is finally time for a new maplibre-rs monthly! Or should I say yearly? The last update was 2022 and we already have 2023!\\
The development of maplibre-rs did not come to a total standstill, even though it may look like it did. Actually, we have great news about the project.\\

\\
This release is a big step for MapLibre GL JS! With more than 500 commits, and almost a year in the making, version 3.0.0 is surely our best release yet. Lots of features, performance improvement, bug fixes and a few potentially breaking changes that were necessary, to keep the project…\\

May 23, 2023](https://maplibre.org/news/2023-05-23-maplibre-gl-js-v3/) [**MapLibre Newsletter May 2023** \\
\\
We would like to thank our first sponsor for their continued trust in MapLibre. Many real-world applications have been built with MapLibre. As suggested it is about bringing the Community together. Implementing new ideas is what will make MapLibre thrive further. We want to recognize all of your…\\

We are happy to announce that komoot has joined the MapLibre Sponsorship Program and supports us as a Silver Sponsor! Komoot is an app that lets you find, plan, and share adventures with its easy route planner. Driven by a love for nature, and powered by the outdoor community’s recommendations,\\
\\

We just released version 2.0.0 of . The update comes with the latest version of MapLibre Native (10.0.2) and includes a navigation UI by default. MapLibre Navigation Android is an open source navigation library which allows everyone to build a customized navigation experience on Android.\\
You can easily integrate a lot…\\

Thanks Ian, Luke, and Jérôme for contributing this package. Read more . There is an ongoing discussion if a style…\\

web.title: Using with Mapbox - Martin Tile Server Documentation
web.description: You can add a layer to the map and specify Martin TileJSON endpoint as a vector source URL. You should also specify a source-layer property. For Table Sources ...
web.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://maplibre.org/martin/using-with-mapbox/#using-with-mapbox)

[Edit this page](https://github.com/maplibre/martin/edit/main/docs/content/using-with-mapbox.md "Edit this page")

# Using with Mapbox

[Mapbox GL JS](https://github.com/mapbox/mapbox-gl-js) is a JavaScript library for interactive, customizable vector maps on the web.
Mapbox GL JS v1.x was open source, and it was forked as MapLibre, so [using Martin with Mapbox is similar to MapLibre](https://maplibre.org/martin/using-with-maplibre/).
Mapbox GL JS can accept [MVT vector tiles](https://github.com/mapbox/vector-tile-spec) generated by Martin, and applies [a style](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-style-spec/) to them to draw a map using Web GL.

You can add a layer to the map and specify Martin TileJSON endpoint as a vector source URL.
You should also specify a `source-layer` property.
For [Table Sources](https://maplibre.org/martin/sources-pg-tables/) it is `{table_name}` by default.

```
map.addLayer({
    id: 'points',
    type: 'circle',
    source: {
        type: 'vector',
        url: 'http://localhost:3000/points'
    },
    'source-layer': 'points',
    paint: {
        'circle-color': 'red'
    }
});
```

Back to top

### Filters

#### Tags
